subject: Physics
title: Quantum Gravity Quest
description: A micro-lesson that makes Quantum Gravity Quest usable.

hook.text: Quantum mechanics and general relativity are both spectacularly accurate—and completely incompatible.

buildup.text: Quantum mechanics governs the tiny. General relativity governs the massive. They use different math.

discovery.text: Inside black holes and at the Big Bang, both theories apply. Their clash produces mathematical nonsense.

twist.text: String theory, loop quantum gravity, and others compete—but none has a testable prediction yet.

climax.text: Whoever unifies quantum mechanics and gravity will solve physics' biggest open problem.

punchline.text: Two perfect theories that can't coexist. The ultimate puzzle.

quiz.question: Why is quantum gravity so difficult to achieve?
options: Quantum mechanics and general relativity are mathematically incompatible
Gravity is too weak to study
We don't have big enough particle accelerators
